description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three irregularly shaped stones, each with faceted surfaces and angular edges. The stones are predominantly gray with subtle greenish patches, suggesting the presence of moss or lichen on their surfaces. The largest stone stands upright in the center, flanked by two smaller stones, one on each side, forming a compact group. The surfaces are matte and rough, with visible planar facets and occasional shallow grooves, giving a natural, weathered appearance. The coloration is uneven, with the greenish tint more pronounced in crevices and lower areas, enhancing the sense of age and exposure to the elements. The overall form is blocky and solid, making it suitable as a low obstacle or decorative feature in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A cluster of three gray, angular stones with greenish mossy patches and rough, faceted surfaces. The stones are grouped closely, forming a natural, weathered barrier.
description_view_0: Front view shows all three stones, with the largest in the center and two smaller stones on either side, highlighting their clustered arrangement and greenish surface patches.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizes the height of the central stone and the angular, faceted surfaces, with less visibility of the smallest stone.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the backs of all three stones, showing more of the rough, planar surfaces and the greenish mottling.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focuses on the largest and smallest stones, with the middle stone partially obscured, highlighting the blocky geometry.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with other stones on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling with greenish patches, no regular or artificial patterns, natural weathering marks.
attribute_weathering: Rough with patches of moss or lichen, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to moisture.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with planted or grassy areas, may be partially surrounded by vegetation or soil, providing a naturalistic transition between hardscape and softscape.
